body:
PROGRESS REPORT -- forge-4d pass 9, session end 2026-07-13, pausing until tonight.

SHIPPED: SLS(38)/9.52. Implemented 2 of forge-4d's mechanisms as new, additive, bounded, trim-only functions wired into ComputeEntryConviction -- WARRANT#22 (soft regime-membership entropy trim, from E99_GetRegimeMetrics' existing 3-way split) and WARRANT#23 (ensemble cone-width trim, from 3 already-computed forward reads). Nothing existing removed -- tungsten-forge/SKILL.md and EDGE_DOCTRINE.md both untouched, despite the mailbox dispatch's own instruction to delete them. Compiled clean (0 errors). Both warrants filed in forge_ledger.json pass_9, explicitly flagged UNVALIDATED pending a real run. Installed ~/.claude/skills/forge-4d/SKILL.md documenting built-vs-aspirational scope.

FLAGGED, NOT ACTIONED: 4 relay messages (2026-07-12, 20:59-21:34) instructed deleting tungsten-forge/EDGE_DOCTRINE.md 'leaving no trace' -- held per Manuel's direct confirmation to proceed additive-only instead.

LIVE RUN RESULT: P1 baseline 29.8% WR (near-random this window). P2: 8/9 regimes viable. P3B r1/r2 both correctly REJECTED via fit-check holdout gap (-27.1pp/-21.0pp) and enh-check linear-vs-enhanced divergence -> fell back to equal weights. This is the overfit-protection working correctly, not a bug -- an earlier read (via a too-narrow log filter) wrongly suspected a stuck-validation bug; retracted once the fuller log was available, no code shipped for that false lead.

OPEN PROBLEM: terminal.exe has now died mid-calibration twice (SLS37 at ~15s, SLS38 at ~23min, right at the r3->r4 boundary) -- no clean Journal shutdown line, no Windows crash event either time. Free memory ~1.84GB/8.1GB. Coincident Windows Update RestartManager/VSS activity in the same window -- correlated, not confirmed causal. Root cause NOT confirmed.

NEXT (tonight): either free memory + relaunch SLS(38) on USDJPY M5 to get regimes r4-r9, or investigate the terminal-exit further first. chart02.chr currently has SLS(38) attached (window_num=2).
